items.standfirst: Knesset approves measure that has been criticised by European countries and rights groupsIsrael’s parliament has passed a law imposing the death penalty on Palestinians convicted of fatal attacks, a measure sharply criticised as discriminatory by European countries and rights groups.The legislation makes the death penalty the default punishment for Palestinians in the Israeli-occupied West Bank found guilty of intentionally carrying out deadly attacks deemed acts of terrorism by a military court. Continue reading...

Continue reading...

items.title: Israel legalises death penalty for Palestinians convicted of murdering Israelis
items.standfirst: Israel's parliament on Monday passed a law approving the death penalty for Palestinians convicted on terror charges of carrying out deadly attacks on Israelis. The law, which also allows the courts to impose the death penalty on Israeli citizens, has been harshly criticised by Israeli and Palestinian rights groups.
items.content: Israel's parliament on Monday passed a law approving the death penalty for Palestinians convicted on terror charges of carrying out deadly attacks on Israelis. The law, which also allows the courts to impose the death penalty on Israeli citizens, has been harshly criticised by Israeli and Palestinian rights groups.

items.title: Israel passes controversial death penalty law
items.standfirst: Israel has passed a law which will make the death penalty by hanging the default punishment for West Bank Palestinians convicted of murdering Israelis.
items.content: Israel has passed a law which will make the death penalty by hanging the default punishment for West Bank Palestinians convicted of murdering Israelis.

items.standfirst: Michael Rousseau faced mockery for speaking English and not French while addressing fatal LaGuardia airport crashSign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxThe head of Canada’s largest airline is stepping down after his video tribute to pilots killed in a fatal collision became a public relations nightmare for Air Canada, prompting a wave of mockery and indignation at him from both the public and politicians for not speaking French.Air Canada’s CEO, Michael Rousseau, will retire by the end of the third quarter of 2026, the company said on Monday. He will continue to lead the company and serve on the board of directors until that time, the carrier said. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Michigan synagogue attacker was inspired by Hezbollah, FBI says
clusters.neutral_headline: Michigan Synagogue Attack Inspired by Hezbollah

items.title: Michigan synagogue attacker was inspired by Hezbollah, FBI says
items.standfirst: Ayman Ghazali, naturalized US citizen from Lebanon, often consumed content linked to Lebanese group onlineThe assailant who attacked a synagogue in Michigan earlier this month was inspired by Hezbollah, the FBI said on Monday.Jennifer Runyan, head of the FBI’s Detroit field office, announced during a press conference that Ayman Ghazali, 41, had frequently consumed Hezbollah-related content online before the attack. In a video recorded before he drove his truck into Temple Israel in West Bloomfield Township – a north-western suburb of Detroit – on 12 March, Ghazali said he wanted to “kill as many of them as I possibly can”. Continue reading...

In a video recorded before he drove his truck into Temple Israel in West Bloomfield Township – a north-western suburb of Detroit – on 12 March, Ghazali said he wanted to “kill as many of them as I possibly can”. Continue reading...

items.title: TSA workers may receive pay soon. And, Israel plans to expand its invasion of Lebanon
items.standfirst: TSA workers have now been without pay for more than 40 days, as Trump says he has a plan to pay them. And, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu announces plans to expand the invasion of Lebanon.
items.content: TSA workers have now been without pay for more than 40 days, as Trump says he has a plan to pay them. And, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu announces plans to expand the invasion of Lebanon.(Image credit: Danielle Villasana)

items.title: Netanyahu says Israel will widen invasion of southern Lebanon
items.standfirst: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Sunday that Israel’s military will widen its ground campaign in southern Lebanon
items.content: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Sunday that Israel’s military will widen its ground campaign in southern Lebanon

ai_summary: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu announces plans to expand
Invasion of southern Lebanon will be widened, according to Netanyahu
TSA workers may receive pay soon, ending a long period without pay
Israel's military campaign in Lebanon sparks concerns over escalation
Incident is part of a broader conflict between Israel and Hezbollah

clusters.title: Hundreds in Beirut mourn journalists killed in Israeli strike
clusters.neutral_headline: Journalists Killed In Israeli Strike Mourned

items.title: Hundreds in Beirut mourn journalists killed in Israeli strike
items.standfirst: BBC reports from the funerals of three journalists killed by a targeted attack in southern Lebanon.
items.content: BBC reports from the funerals of three journalists killed by a targeted attack in southern Lebanon.

items.title: Hundreds of mourners gather in pouring rain for funerals of Lebanon journalists
items.standfirst: Hundreds of mourners turned out in the pouring rain in Beirut for the funerals of three journalists killed by Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon.
items.content: Hundreds of mourners turned out in the pouring rain in Beirut for the funerals of three journalists killed by Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon.

ai_summary: Three journalists killed in southern Lebanon
Hundreds mourn in Beirut
Israeli strike targeted journalists
Funerals held in pouring rain
Attack condemned by international community
